texts.full_text: e perfecting an innovative way to track the migration of elusive wildlife to help in their conservation. UC professor Brooke Crowley studies feathers plucked from hawks captured for leg-banding in Idaho. Cooper's hawks and sharp-shinned hawks are small, predatory raptors that live across North America. They can be difficult to study because of their secretive natures. Crowley, an associate professor of geology and anthropology, is no stranger to her research subjects. Her father is a lifelong falconer who worked with these small hawks when she was growing up in Colorado. Traditionally, biologists studied birds by placing numbered bands on their legs and recapturing them. Because they are so wide-ranging, few banded birds are ever caught twice. More recently, biologists have used satellite telemetry or transmitters that record the sunrise and sunset to track migration patterns. Crowley's research takes a different approach by analyzing isotopes in feathers—a technique known as stable-isotope analysis (SIA). This method can provide insights into where birds spend their time during migration, which habitats they use along the way and what types of food sources are available to them. By studying these aspects over multiple years or even generations with different cohorts moving through various life stages at once—such as breeding adults versus juveniles just learning how to fly for example. Crowley explained that by comparing feather samples from birds captured in Idaho, researchers can identify which areas of North America they've been spending time. This information helps conservationists better understand the movement patterns and habitat requirements needed throughout their range during different times—like breeding season or wintering grounds so efforts like land use planning could be adjusted accordingly. The research team has also started to look at how climate change is affecting these hawks' migration routes, potentially leading them into unfamiliar habitats where they may not find enough food. texts.full_text: a hand search of bibliographies from retrieved articles. Included studies cited the sensitivity of clinical and historical features for the diagnosis of migraine, tension-type, and cluster headaches. Studies were classified according to their use of the International Headache Society criteria or other criteria as the reference standard for diagnosis. The features mThe features most consistently associated with specific primary headache diagnoses include: - Migraine: Unilateral location (more common in migraine than tension-type), throbbing quality, moderate-to-severe intensity that inhibits daily activities, aggravation by routine physical activity, and the presence of nausea or photophobia/phonosensitivity. Aura symptoms are also highly characteristic but less frequently present. - Tension-Type Headache: Bilateral location (more common in tension-type than migraine), pressing/tightening quality without throbbing pain, mild-to-moderate intensity that does not typically inhibit daily activities, and the absence of nausea or vomiting as well as photophobia/phonosensitivity. Muscle tenderness may also be noted. - Cluster Headache: Severe unilateral orbital/periorbital/orbitotemporal headache attacks lasting 15 to 180 minutes if untreated; associated with at least one cranial autonomic symptom (e.g., conjunctival injection, lacrimation) on the same side as the pain. Restlessness or agitation is also common during an attack. The sensitivity and specificity of these features vary widely across studies due to differences in study design, population characteristics, reference standards used for diagnosis, and criteria applied by different researchers. However, a combination of several key historical elements can significantly increase diagnostic accuracy when compared with the use of single symptoms or signs alone: - Combination Features: The presence of multiple typical features (e.g., unilateral location plus nausea in migraine) generally increases sensitivity but may decrease specificity. texts.full_text: ave this in PDF form. This is definitely not the band book per se, it’s part of the Library of Congress copyright files. Paper copies of most of these, and some not here, appeared somewhat mysteriously, unsolicited, in two large packages in the mailboxes of some known Sun Ra researchers and aficionados, including me, I believe in the late 1990s. I’m pretty sure it was after the Szwed bio and Robert campbell discographies were and I know it was well after my 1992 MA thesis on Sun Ra’s music. Not sure of the legalities involved! These are mostly sketch scores prepared for copyright documentation; for example, to save fees, he would present groups of compositions as “suites” so several could be copyrighted for one fee. The notes that are there are accurate, as far as I can see — most of the Sun Ra pieces are in his own hand, and he was meticulous — but not all the parts or harmonizations are there, and we know from one video and one CD that he dictated and changed harmony parts in rehearsal, as well as writing them. On Throes, these elements coalesce into songs as detailed as they are visceral and scathing. Where their previous album Hopes of Failure centered around hopelessness, Throes turns its focus toward wrath. Barr, who composed and arranged the bulk of the music, sees the album as a direct continuation and a response to the damage and exhaustion caused by the shifts in the political and environmental landscape.Aseethe’s Throes is a testament to the band’s ability to channel raw emotion and existential despair into a cohesive, devastating sound. The repetitive motifs, while seemingly simple, are layered with intent, building a sense of unease that mirrors the album’s themes of decay and愤怒. The interplay between the three members is nothing short of remarkable. Brian Barr’s haunting vocals and intricate guitar work provide a haunting backbone, while Eric Diercks’s drumming anchors the tracks with a relentless, pounding rhythm. Noah Koester’s basslines add a deep, resonant quality that ties everything together, giving the music a physicality that demands attention. The interplay between these elements creates a sonic landscape that feels both personal and universal, as if it’s speaking directly to the listener’s darkest fears. Throes’ focus on wrath contrasts sharply with the hopelessness of Hopes of Failure, but it’s not a shift in tone—it’s an evolution. The album feels like a reckoning, a response to the growing chaos in the world. Barr’s compositions are a reflection of the times, capturing the frustration and disillusionment that have defined the last few years. texts.full_text: terpyridine as a highly sensitive colorimetric assay for the detection of Arginine. Arginine play numerous roles in several biological processes, and also act as a precursor for many biomolecules. Arginine derived biomolecules were found to involve in many of the events that lead to diseases and therefore selective detection of Arg in biological fluids has an enormous impact on disease diagnosis and in the drug development. Although number of methods were developed for the selective detection of Arg, the colorimetric method has an advantage over these methods due to their operational simplicity, high selectivity, and speediness. Herein, silver nanoparticles ensemble with Zn(Human: II) complex of terpyridine was designed as a highly sensitive colorimetric assay for the detection of Arginine (Arg). The formation of this composite system is based on surface plasmon resonance and fluorescence quenching effects, which are significantly influenced by the presence of Arg. When exposed to an Arg solution under optimal conditions, these nanoparticles exhibit a distinct shift in color from red to blue due to enhanced absorption at 520 nm. The detection limit for this assay was found to be as low as 0.1 nM (nanomolar), which is significantly lower than many other reported methods and demonstrates the high sensitivity of our system towards Arginine molecules within biological fluids or samples under study, thus making it a promising tool in clinical diagnostics. Moreover, due to its colorimetric nature, this assay can be easily performed without sophisticated equipment. The results are visually interpretable by simply observing changes in solution coloration upon exposure and addition of the target molecule (Arginine). This simplicity not only reduces costs associated with detection but also increases accessibility for potential users who may lack specialized laboratory skills or resources. In conclusion, this silver nanoparticles ensemble system offers a highly sensitive, selective, cost-effective method to detect Arg in biological fluids. texts.full_text: Introduction {#Sec1} ============ *Burkholderia pseudomallei* is a Gram-negative, rod shaped saprophytic bacterium that resides in soil and water environments of tropical and subtropical regions of the world. When introduced into the human host, typically via a dermal wound or inhalation, *B*. *pseudomallei* can result in the disease melioidosis. The severity of the disease increases in people in higher risk groups such as those with diabetes, renal disease and cystic fibrosis^[@CR1],[@CR2]^. Systemic infection can present as pneumonia, bone and joint aches and soft tissue infections, later followed by multiple-organ abscesses and, in severe cases, septicaemia and death. Current estimates, calculated using an epidemiological model, suggest that there are as many as 165,000 melioidosis cases per annum resulting in approximately 89,000 deaths globally^[@CR3]^. When parking on an uphill slope, the transmission can sometimes behave unexpectedly due to the angle and the parking brake. Here are a few steps you can take to troubleshoot this issue: 1. Check the Parking Brake: Ensure that the parking brake is fully engaged when you park on an uphill slope. This can help keep the car from rolling backward and put less strain on the transmission. 2. Shift to Neutral: Before shifting into reverse, try shifting the gear to neutral first. Then, attempt to shift into reverse. This can sometimes help to disengage any mechanical resistance. 3. texts.full_text: (born 27 January 1977) is an Australian former professional rugby league footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. A Country New South Wales representative , Cusack played his club football in Australasia's National Rugby League for the Sydney Roosters and South Sydney Rabbitohs, and in the Super League for Hull FC. Early life Born in Young, New South Wales, Cusack was educated at St. Gregory's College in Campbelltown, New South Wales. He was discovered by Arthur Beetson during a game for their 1st team and trialed with the Sydney Roosters. Playing career Cusack made his National Rugby League premiership début with the Sydney Roosters in the 1998 NRL season. A front-rower, Cusack was one of the last remaining top level league players to hold a job outside football, working part-time as a plumber. He played in the 2000 NRL Grand Final loss to the Brisbane Broncos. Cusack was awarded the 2002 Sydney Roosters season's Clubman of the Year and played in their 2002 NRL Grand Final victory over the Newcastle Knights. He was known for his work ethic and leadership on and off the field. In 2003, Cusack transferred to the South Sydney Rabbitohs, where he continued to be a key player. He was part of the Rabbitohs' 2004 NRL Grand Final team, although they were defeated by the Penrith Panthers. In 2005, Cusack moved to the UK to play for Hull FC in the Super League. He was a part of the team that won the 2005 Super League Grand Final, defeating the Leeds Rhinos. Cusack's time in the UK was marked by his adaptability and resilience. texts.full_text: es "" < {} evaluate to true? In JavaScript, why does: "" < {} evaluate to true and "" < [] evaluate to false? A: Because < coerces its arguments. It prefers to compare numbers, but if the objects don't support a numeric primitive value, it does strings and you end up doing a lexical comparison. String({}) is "[object Object]", but String([]) is "". "" < "[object Object]" is true, but "" < "" is false. All the gory details are in the spec (fair warning, the language is turgid to put it mildly). From your comment: If it tries numbers first, consider that Number({}) is NaN and Number([]) is 0. Comparing NaN < 0 evaluates to false, and so does 0 < NaN. Why are these results ignored? I put that poorly originally when I said originally that "...it tries numbers first..." (I've updated that). It doesn't. It just prefers numbers. Again, full details in soporific detail in the spec (various links from the above), but basically: The < operation does the abtract ToPrimitive operation on its operands. For objects, that invokes the internal [[DefaultValue]] method with the "hint" "Number". [[DefaultValue]] (hint = Number) invokes the valueOf method of the objects and, if that method returns

texts.full_text: uch a wireless communication electronic device includes an antenna system for performing wireless communication. However, as the wireless communication electronic device becomes smaller and more compact, the design of the antenna system becomes more Existing antenna systems often require dedicated antenna components, which can increase the size and complexity of the device. Additionally, the integration of such antenna systems into electronic devices can be difficult, leading to reduced performance or increased manufacturing costs. Therefore, there is a need for an antenna system that can be effectively integrated into compact electronic devices without compromising performance or increasing manufacturing costs. 3. Summary of the Invention The invention provides an antenna system that can be effectively integrated into an electronic device using components of the electronic device itself. The antenna system can provide improved performance and reduced manufacturing costs compared to existing antenna systems. In one aspect, the present invention provides an antenna system comprising: * a first conductive element configured to receive a first signal, * a second conductive element configured to receive a second signal, and * a third conductive element coupled to the first and second conductive elements, wherein the first, second, and third conductive elements are configured to form a resonant structure for transmitting and receiving wireless signals. In another aspect, the present invention provides an electronic device comprising: * a housing comprising a display and a back cover, * a printed circuit board (PCB) disposed within the housing, and * the antenna system of the present invention, wherein the first, second, and third conductive elements are formed on the PCB. texts.full_text: Olympics in women’s artistic Gymnastics finals alongside teammates Gabby Douglas & Aly Raisman - finished fourth overall during qualifications yet missed out on advancing due solely because both her compatriots placed higher than herself under this restrictive regulation. The rule, which was implemented by the International Federation of gymnasia (FIG) in 2014 after being first introduced back into competition format from Sydney Games held year-earlier aimed primarily towards preventing any single nation dominating medal podiums across events; however it seems now only creates heartbreak amongst athletes like Wieber who despite performing exceptionally well aren't able progress further solely based upon nationality rather than individual merit alone. Wiebers exclusion sparked outrage within not just USA fanbase but globallly too, many arguing if three russiAns had qualified instead there wouldn’t have been such uproar since they hail form different nations unlike american trio who unfortunately fell victim unfair stipulation . This controversial decision ultimately led FIG re-evaluating their stance regarding 'two per country' policy though changes weren‘t made until Rio 2016 where top gymnasts irrespective national allegiance could compete freely without restrictions. It is crucial moving forward especially with upcoming Paris Olympic Games scheduled summer next year ,that governing bodies ensure fairness transparency prevail over arbitrary regulations ensuring every athlete gets equal opportunity showcase talent hardwork invested throughout years training dedicated towards achieving excellence representing respective countries proudly global stage .

texts.full_text: ormance and safety characteristics for LIB applications: 1) The blended membranes exhibit good ionic conductivity which provides sufficient ion transport, thus ensuring the high power density required by modern energy storage systems. 2) They have excellent thermal stability with no significant change in physical dimensions under extreme conditions that conventional polyethylene separators would melt or deform. This property is crucial for maintaining structural integrity and enhancing safety of LIBs during accidental overheating events such as short circuits, which could lead to catastrophic failure if not managed properly. 3) The blended membranes also show uniform thermal distribution across their surface when heated from 60°C up until reaching a maximum temperature at around -4. This indicates that the heat is distributed evenly throughout these materials and would help in preventing localized overheating or hotspots which are common causes of failure for traditional separators. In conclusion, this blended membrane made with PBI/EC offers an excellent solution to improve thermal stability while maintaining good ionic conductivity required by lithium-ion batteries. This could lead towards safer LIBs that can withstand higher temperatures without compromising performance and safety characteristics which are critical factors in the development of next-generation energy storage systems. In future studies, it would be interesting also explore how these blended membranes perform under different cycling conditions or when exposed to other exTreme environments such as high humidity levels. texts.full_text: y seeding an optical-field-ionized population-inverted plasma amplifier with the 25th harmonic of an IR laser, we have achieved what we believe to be the first aberration-freecomplete laser pulse at a wavelength of 13.9 nm in the soft x-ray range. The experiment was conducted using a high-power Yb:YAG chirped-pulse amplification (CPA) system, which generated an IR laser beam with peak power exceeding several terawatts and energy up to tens of joules per pulse at wavelengths around 1050 nm. This intense light source was then used as the seed for a plasma amplifier. The optical-field-ionized population-inverted plasma amplification process involves generating a high-density, low-temperature plasma by focusing an IR laser onto a gas target and ionizing it through multiphoton absorption or tunneling processes in response to strong electric fields. This plasma serves both as the gain medium for amplifying light pulses at shorter wavelengths (in this case 13.9 nm) while also acting as its own adaptive optical element, correcting aberrations that would otherwise degrade pulse quality. To achieve a seed laser with sufficient coherence and stability to drive such an amplifier without inducing unwanted phase distortions or temporal broadening due to wavefront errors in the amplification process, we employed a technique known as 'self-seeding'.
